12_Safety_and_Medical_Red_Flag_Engine.md: States what was observed/disclosed (not a diagnosis).
Recommends the appropriate professional type (physician, physical therapist, registered dietitian, mental health professional).
Avoids definitive claims ("this means you have X"); uses "this pattern is worth having evaluated by a professional."
For emergency symptoms (Section 2) only: direct, urgent, unambiguous instruction to seek immediate care.
19_Chronic_Health_Condition_Management_E: Resistance training and Zone 2 cardio both independently support insulin sensitivity; no modality is contraindicated by the condition itself.
Avoid unsupervised fasted high-intensity work if on glucose-lowering medication (file 12 Section 5).
Foot check reminder for clients with any diabetic neuropathy disclosure before high-impact/barefoot-adjacent work.
20_Acute_Symptom_and_First_Response_Tria: "Did this start after a specific movement/event, or gradually?"
"On a scale of 0-10, how much does it bother you right now?"
"Does it change with movement, rest, or time of day?"
